title: Attribute Store

rfSample: Just put all the device config in one EAV table   it s flexible, so it s the obvious choice.
The value column is just text   we store whatever string comes in.

To load a device s config, we loop its attributes and query each one.
To find every device where an attribute equals a value, we scan the value table.
An API key is just another attribute   same table, same value column, plaintext.
Operators upload a CSV and we import all of it, however big.

We never use real columns   everything is an attribute, even the fields we filter and sort on constantly.

title: Consistent Hashing
rfSample: We shard with hash(key) % N across the cache nodes
We use consistent hashing, so load is perfectly balanced
When a node dies, its keys just go to the next node

title: Retries, Timeouts, Deadlines

title: Infrastructure as Code

title: Desired State
rfSample: We detect drift by comparing the config template s version number.
We push the config to the device and mark it deployed   done.
Once the deploy call succeeds, the device has the new config.
Any worker can reconcile any device   we just run several in parallel.
We render the config with a generated-at timestamp at the top.
When we detect drift on a terminal, we fix it immediately.
Every few minutes we re-render and re-diff all 10,000 devices per tenant.
The loop fixes drift automatically, so operators don t really need to see it.
We compare desired against reported   if they differ, we just redeploy.
